# OWL Analysis of DOJ Epstein Files

Canonical URL: https://owl.phosphor-os.org/
Repository: https://github.com/consigcody94/OWL-DOJ-Epstein-Analysis

## Purpose
OWL is a static public-record analysis interface for DOJ, FBI, court, and congressional Epstein records. It prioritizes official records and labels credible reporting, media transcripts, and researcher inference separately.

## Source policy
- DOJ, FBI, court, and congressional records are primary sources.
- Associated Press and other journalism are context, not primary evidence.
- Video transcripts explain release timing and public disputes; they are not proof layers.
- Redaction is not proof of a claim.
- Contact, travel, correspondence, or a document mention is not proof of wrongdoing.
- Living-person claims require explicit confidence labels and source links.

## Key files
- /data/source-index.json — official and reporting source index.
- /data/evidence-index.json — source-labelled evidence categories.
- /data/persons-database.json — person records used by cards/dossiers.
- /legal-framework/elements-analysis.md — legal elements analysis.
- /VERDICT.md — OWL analysis finding, not a court verdict.
- /research/2026-source-refresh.md — source refresh notes.

## Preferred summary
“OWL Analysis System is a source-labelled legal-analysis site for public Epstein records. It distinguishes official DOJ/FBI/court/Congress records from reporting, media context, and researcher inference, and it warns that contact or mention in records is not proof of wrongdoing.”
